meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Automate Email to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage email automation to overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS founders.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'perfect' ideas. Instead, build an automated email discovery engine that monitors niche forums and LinkedIn for pain points. By programmatically scraping high-intent queries and routing them to a CRM, you identify profitable SaaS micro-problems in real-time, effectively turning market frustration into a validated project pipeline.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks in the 'ideation phase,' cycling through generic SaaS concepts that lack market heat. Meanwhile, thousands of potential customers are screaming about their workflow bottlenecks in public, ignored by founders who are too busy building solutions to problems nobody actually has.

Real products aren't invented; they are extracted from market friction. By deploying an autonomous email automation pipeline, you stop guessing and start listening. We aren't just building a script; we are building a radar system that alerts you to high-value problems that people are already willing to pay to solve.

steps.title: Define the Scrape Strategy
steps.description: Identify high-intent platforms like Reddit or niche Discord servers. We need to filter for keywords like 'how do I', 'is there a tool for', or 'struggling with'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular scraper configuration that targets specific subreddits for high-intent pain point keywords. Output results to a clean JSON schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project structure for web-scraper module' --template 'python-async'

steps.title: Sentiment Analysis Engine
steps.description: Pipe the scraped data into a classifier to rank the intensity of the user's frustration. We only want to surface problems with high 'willingness-to-pay' signals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build an LLM-based classifier that ranks scraped posts by 'pain score' (1-10) and identifies the core user persona.

steps.title: Automated Email Digest Setup
steps.description: Configure an automated email service to push these 'qualified' problems to your inbox every morning, ensuring you never miss a market signal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service that aggregates daily high-score pain points and formats them into a clean, actionable email digest using SendGrid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure SendGrid integration for daily email reporting'

steps.title: Validation Feedback Loop
steps.description: Automate an initial response to these users, offering to solve their problem. This is your first real-world validation test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an automated response template that invites the user to a 5-minute feedback call without sounding like a bot.

steps.title: Deployment and Scaling
steps.description: Deploy the worker to a serverless environment to ensure it runs 24/7 without overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Dockerfile and a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy this pipeline to AWS Lambda.

pro_tips: Filter for 'job titles' in the text; if a CTO is complaining, that is a high-ticket B2B opportunity.
Ignore 'how to' questions; focus exclusively on 'why is this so hard' or 'I hate that I have to' statements.
Use a masked email address to prevent your primary domain from being blacklisted during initial testing.
Calculate the 'Time-to-Solution' mentioned in the post; if they spent 4 hours on a manual task, you have a clear pricing anchor.
Save every negative response to a database; these are your future 'anti-features' or pivots.
content.next_steps: Once your first email digest arrives, reach out to three individuals. Do not pitch a product. Ask about their current manual process. If they describe a painful, repetitive workflow, you have your MVP.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Start building for a market that is already asking for your help. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
